_publications._publicationTitle: Patient-Derived Tumor Organoid Rings for Histologic Characterization and High-Throughput Screening
_publications._context: Protocol for generating patient-derived tumor organoids in a ring geometry enabling media exchange, histopathologic analysis, and high-throughput drug screening

_publications._publicationTitle: A platform for rapid patient-derived cutaneous neurofibroma organoid establishment and screening
_publications._context: Patient-derived cNF organoids closely recapitulate cellular and molecular features of parental tumors

basicInfo.description: Patient-derived cutaneous neurofibroma organoids in a ring geometry using Matrigel that recapitulate cellular and molecular features of parental NF1 tumors; used for histologic characterization and high-throughput drug screening
basicInfo.synonyms: cNF organoids; cNF organoid rings; mini-ring organoids; mini-rings; maxi-rings
basicInfo.cultureMedia: StemPro-34 SFM supplemented with 1X N-2, 10 ng/mL Neuregulin, 100 ng/mL rhSCF, 50 ng/mL rhIL-6, 1 ng/mL rhIL-3, and optional 2 μM Forskolin; development protocol used MammoCult Human Medium Kit with hydrocortisone and heparin
basicInfo.cryopreservationProtocol: Cells are spun at 1,000g for 5 min, resuspended in Recovery Cell Culture Freezing Medium (Thermo Fisher Scientific #12648010), transferred to a cryotube and frozen at −80°C
qualityControlMetrics: Spearman rank correlation >0.8 comparing organoid transcriptome to parental tumor
Flow cytometry cell population matching to parental lesion (Schwann cells 19–48%, fibroblasts 18–35%, mast cells 3–9%)
Methylation Pearson correlation 0.69–0.72 between organoids and primary tissue
Homogeneous ring morphology with no broken rings or bubbles

modelLimitations: Lacks axonal elements of the tumor microenvironment, which may influence tumor growth; screening results focused on kinase inhibitors with limited assessment of drug combination therapies; ring quality is operator-dependent
